summary: Tuples are immutable sequences created with `()`. Require trailing comma for 1-element tuples `(x,)`. Usable as dict keys if hashable.
oneLineSummary: Tuples are immutable fixed-size sequences ideal for records and dict keys.

memoryTricks.trick: Tuple = Fixed Lockbox. Once shut, you can't swap what's inside.

commonErrors.error: TypeError: 'tuple' object does not support item assignment

preInterviewChecklist: Explain why single-element tuple needs comma

nextTopics.title: List Comprehensions
nextTopics.whyNext: Learn concise syntax for constructing lists.
